device_name: Venue; Venue Go; Venue Fit; Venue Sprint
company: GE Medical Systems Ultrasound and Primary care Diagnostics,
intended_use: General purpose diagnostic ultrasound systems for use by qualified and trained healthcare professionals or practitioners that are legally authorized or licensed by law in the country, state or other l
intended_use_full: General purpose diagnostic ultrasound systems for use by qualified and trained healthcare professionals or practitioners that are legally authorized or licensed by law in the country, state or other local municipality in which he or she practices, for ultrasound imaging, measurement, display and analysis of the human body and fluid. Clinical applications include: abdominal (GYN and Urology), thoracic/pleural, ophthalmic, Fetal/OB, Small Organ (including breast, testes, thyroid), Vascular/Peripheral vascular, neonatal and adult cephalic, pediatric, musculoskeletal (conventional and superficial), cardiac (adults and pediatric), Transrectal, Transvaginal, Transesophageal, Intraoperative (vascular) and interventional guidance (includes tissue biopsy, fluid drainage, vascular and non-vascular access).
target_condition: Bladder volume assessment; cardiac systolic and atrial function assessment
ground_truth: Manual annotation of 4-6 representative images per subject by annotators who placed 4 landmark points representing bladder edges (Transverse: Anterior, Posterior, left, right; Longitudinal: Head, Foot, Anterior, Posterior) corresponding to caliper placement locations for bladder volume measurement
other_metrics: Auto Bladder Volume - Automatic caliper placement success rate: 95.09% (95% confidence level); Success rates by BMI: healthy weight (18.5-24.9): 95.64%, Obese (25-29.9): 95.59%, overweight (over 30): 92.6%
